### Post by retrorock on 2013-07-08

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/;base64,[/IMG]

[http://www.flickr.com/photos/98631509@N04/9245284352/](http://www.flickr.com/photos/98631509@N04/9245284352/)

This is my roommate's monitor plugged into ubuntu using the same DVI-D cable: 
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/;base64,[/IMG]
[IMG]http://www.flickr.com/photos/98631509@N04/9242500181/in/photostream/[/IMG]
[http://www.flickr.com/photos/98631509@N04/9242500181/in/photostream/](http://www.flickr.com/photos/98631509@N04/9242500181/in/photostream/)

Yes I used the same cable. I did not change any settings between the two screens. When I plug my monitor into my windows computer, picture looks fine. I have no idea what to do to fix this. I tried going onto my monitor's manufacture's website and I downloaded the color profile and I clicked on it and it said "Color profile has been imported." But no change and I tried changing the resolution but no change and I even messed with the monitor's buttons but no change I even tried changing cables but no change. Anyone have any ideas?

### Post by coldraven on 2013-07-09
Was it ever working correctly or is this a new monitor?
The ultimate test would be to see if it works with a different PC, maybe using Windows.

---

### Post by jp734 on 2013-07-09
Color depth setting maybe? Is your configuration set to 8 or 16? Might need to change to 24 or 32.
